hero.question: Will Connecticut win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 13.9%, the crowd is essentially saying Connecticut has no realistic path to a national title, which is aggressive given they consistently make the tournament and Dan Hurley has proven he can win in March. The real question is whether you're buying a program that's genuinely broken or a discount on a coach who just went to back-to-back Elite Eights, and the current price doesn't adequately price in tournament variance.

daily_take.headline: Connecticut's Tournament Odds Stay Soft Despite $3M in Volume
daily_take.deck: Connecticut sits at 13.9% to win the 2026 NCAA Tournament, up a tick over 24 hours on $3M in volume, but the market is overvaluing recency bias around a single season. The Huskies have real talent but no Final Four resume yet, and 14% prices in championship-level consistency they haven't proven. Sharp money should be fading the hype and looking at teams with deeper tournament track records at similar odds.

sidebar.headline: López Chau's Peruvian play collapsed 2.5 points in 24 hours, signaling either new polling or conviction bets against him ahead of critical endorsement windows.

sidebar.headline: Arizona's 32% is climbing fast on $1.8M volume, but ask yourself if one tournament run justifies odds that rival programs with deeper experience.
sidebar.label: Arizona 2026 Tournament: 32.2%
